name: MidWest Foldable Metal Dog Exercise Pen, 4 Panel 36-Inch
whatWorks: The accordion fold design collapses to a flat stack in seconds without tools, making it far more practical for temporary containment setups than pen systems that require assembly. Owners who set up and break down the pen daily — for puppy containment during work hours — can do it in under 30 seconds each way.

The 36-inch height stops most medium breeds from jumping out while remaining low enough to step over easily for humans. The included walk-through gate panel adds a proper entry point so you're not lifting yourself over the pen multiple times a day, which becomes important over months of use.

Panel versatility lets you configure a straight barrier, a square pen, an L-shape, or attach to a crate opening to extend the crated area. For puppy management in particular, the ability to gate off a kitchen section or extend a crate's safe zone makes this more useful than a fixed baby gate.

Powder-coated wire resists rust and cleans with a wipe — relevant for outdoor use in rain or when puppies have accidents inside the pen. The coating holds up well to normal outdoor exposure through Canadian seasons without significant corrosion.
worthKnowing: Determined large-breed dogs can push this pen across smooth floors because the legs don't anchor to the ground. Placing the pen against a wall, weighting the base with a heavy mat, or purchasing ground stakes for outdoor use is necessary for dogs that lean into barriers.

36-inch height is not jump-proof for athletic medium breeds or any large breed — a motivated retriever, shepherd, or husky will clear 36 inches without effort. This pen is appropriate for puppies, small breeds, and non-athletic dogs, not for containing active adult dogs that can jump.
The wire panels can be climbed by puppies that figure out the footholds on the wire mesh. Some owners add fabric panel inserts or purchase pen covers to address this, at additional cost.

Panel configuration is limited to straight lines and 90-degree corners — you can't create curved enclosures or acute angles. For specific room shapes this limits how well the pen fits the available space.

commonComplaints.title: My lab jumped it at 5 months old…
commonComplaints.body: At 5 months my lab was already clearing the 36-inch panels without any effort. This is a puppy pen for small to medium breeds or very young large-breed puppies. For a grown lab you need the 42-inch version.

commonComplaints.title: Slides on my hardwood floor…
commonComplaints.body: My golden pushes against it and slides the whole pen across the room in about 10 minutes. I had to press it against the wall and put a heavy rug under it. It holds now but that shouldn't be required.

commonComplaints.title: Puppy climbed out through the wire footholds…
commonComplaints.body: My 12-week cockapoo figured out that the wire mesh is climbable within two days. I had to drape a blanket over the inside panels to remove the footholds. Works fine now but wasn't expecting to need a modification.

commonComplaints.title: Only 4 panels limits configuration options…
commonComplaints.body: With 4 panels the square configuration is only 24x24 inches — fine for a puppy but not enough room for an adult small dog. I ended up buying a second set to connect together for a bigger space.

verdict: The MidWest Foldable Metal Exercise Pen is the right value choice for temporary puppy containment, small-breed management, or creating a dog-safe zone in a specific room. Its instant fold-and-store design and gate panel make daily use practical. Height and anchor limitations mean it's not a solution for athletic medium-to-large breeds or determined climbers — understand the use case before buying.

realBuyers.quote: I set this up every morning around my puppy's crate to give her space to play while I work, and fold it flat every evening. The whole operation takes under a minute. It's been essential for the first three months.
Perfect for containing my Shih Tzu in the kitchen during dinner parties. Sets up in 20 seconds, folds flat in 10. Guests are always surprised it fits behind the door when not in use.
I use this outdoors to give my small dog a secure play area when I'm working in the garden. Ground stakes keep it stable and she can't jump the 36-inch height. Works exactly as intended.
Bought this for my corgi puppy and it's been in daily use for five months. Easy to clean, folds flat under the bed, and she's never gotten out. For a 15-pound dog this pen is the right product.

metaDescription: Dog exercise pen review for Canada: 4 panels, 36-inch height, accordion fold, gate panel, indoor/outdoor. Honest on jumping and sliding limitations. ~$49 CAD.
introText: This foldable metal dog exercise pen provides flexible containment with 4 wire panels, a walk-through gate, and an accordion fold that collapses to a flat stack in seconds — practical for puppy management and small-breed containment indoors or outdoors. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07MJ2S9F8) at around $49 CAD.
